mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales signals robust consumer expenditure which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when RSAFS is in its top quartile of historical growth, or the Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day returns is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market tightening and household income growth, which boosts aggregate demand and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE declines more than 0.5pp in 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances, or the mean return in triggered periods is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and a low federal funds rate below 1% creates a supply-friendly liquidity environment where manufacturing expansion is not constrained by borrowing costs, producing strongly positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO growth and FEDFUNDS below 1% fails to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of historical observations, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) beyond individual series in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, elevated CPI inflation above 3% year-over-year combined with an unemployment rate below 5.5% signals an overheating economy that forces prospective monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3% and UNRATE is below 5.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the Sharpe ratio of a short position in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Spearman correlation between INDPRO changes and 5-day returns is negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustained above 1,200 thousand units indicate a strong construction sector and durable goods demand that reverberates through supplier industries, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1200, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of historical months, or the average 60-day return in triggered periods is less than the unconditional average return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income and consumption expenditures, which raises corporate top-line growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the information coefficient between PAYEMS growth and 20-day returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25%, accelerating CPI inflation reduces real interest rates and pushes investors out of cash and fixed income into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL is rising, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, or a regression of 20-day returns on the interaction of low FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL changes yields a negative coefficient that is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05).
